title: 魚へんの漢字
copy: 辞書上の部首が魚に分類されるJIS第一・第二水準の漢字。

items.name: Horse mackerel
items.meaning: Horse mackerel; a common food fish known as aji in Japanese.

items.name: Sweetfish
items.meaning: Sweetfish; a river fish known as ayu in Japanese.
items.note: In Japanese usage, 鮎 commonly refers to ayu or sweetfish.

items.name: Sardine
items.meaning: Sardine; a small fish commonly called iwashi in Japanese.

items.name: Eel
items.meaning: Eel; a familiar food fish known as unagi in Japanese.

items.name: Loach or bullhead
items.meaning: A fish kanji with Japanese readings for loach, young yellowtail, and bullhead.
items.note: The Japanese readings point to different fish names, so the public gloss stays broad.

items.name: Bonito
items.meaning: Bonito or skipjack tuna; a fish known as katsuo in Japanese.

items.name: Fish
items.meaning: Fish; a general kanji for fish.

items.name: Whale
items.meaning: Whale; a large sea mammal known as kujira in Japanese.
items.note: Although written with the fish radical, whales are mammals.

items.name: Carp
items.meaning: Carp; a freshwater fish known as koi in Japanese.

items.name: Salmon
items.meaning: Salmon; a fish commonly called sake or shake in Japanese.

items.name: Mackerel
items.meaning: Mackerel; a fish known as saba in Japanese.

items.name: Shark
items.meaning: Shark; a fish known as same in Japanese.

items.name: Fresh or vivid
items.meaning: Fresh, vivid, clear, or brilliant; a common non-fish meaning under the fish radical.
items.note: Common Japanese use is adjectival, as in fresh or vivid, rather than a fish name.

items.name: Sea bream
items.meaning: Sea bream; a fish known as tai in Japanese.

items.name: Cod
items.meaning: Cod; a fish known as tara in Japanese.

items.name: Fin
items.meaning: Fin; a fish body part known as hire in Japanese.

items.name: Crucian carp
items.meaning: Crucian carp; a freshwater fish known as funa in Japanese.

items.name: Tuna
items.meaning: Tuna; a fish known as maguro in Japanese.

items.name: Trout
items.meaning: Trout; a fish known as masu in Japanese.

items.name: Scale
items.meaning: Scale; the scales of fish, reptiles, and other animals.

items.name: Lu or foolish
items.meaning: Lu as a historical proper name, and foolish or dull in older usage.
items.note: This fish-radical character is not a fish name in common Japanese use.

items.name: Crocodile
items.meaning: Crocodile or alligator; a reptile known as wani in Japanese.
items.note: Older Japanese usage can also refer to sharks or other large dangerous fish.

items.name: Bream or John Dory
items.meaning: A fish kanji associated with bream or John Dory; Japanese readings include kagamidai and oshikiuo.
items.note: The Japanese entry lists readings but leaves the definition unfilled, so the English gloss follows the fish glosses on the same source page.

items.name: Sushi
items.meaning: Sushi; an older or variant kanji spelling for vinegared rice served with fish, vegetables, or other toppings.
items.note: Used as an alternative spelling of sushi and closely related to 鮨.

items.name: Olive flounder
items.meaning: Olive flounder; a flatfish known as hirame in Japanese.
items.note: Alternative spellings include 平目 and 比目魚.

items.name: Abalone
items.meaning: Abalone; an edible marine mollusk known as awabi in Japanese.
items.note: Although written with the fish radical, abalone is a mollusk.

items.name: Kajika sculpin
items.meaning: Kajika; a freshwater sculpin in the family Cottidae.
items.note: Kanjipedia notes that the character evokes a fish living among stones.

items.name: Dotted gizzard shad
items.meaning: Dotted gizzard shad; a fish known as konoshiro in Japanese.
items.note: This kokuji is mainly tied to konoshiro, with a rarer irregular hiiragi use also listed.

items.name: Anglerfish or monkfish
items.meaning: Anglerfish or monkfish; used in compounds such as 鮟鱇 for anko.
items.note: Only found in compounds; the familiar fish name is 鮟鱇.

items.name: Dace
items.meaning: Dace; a freshwater carp-family fish read haya or hae in Japanese.
items.note: Chinese and Korean senses point to catfish, but the Japanese entry gives dace.

items.name: Sushi
items.meaning: Sushi; a kanji spelling for vinegared rice served with fish, vegetables, or other toppings.
items.note: Listed as an alternative spelling of the same sushi entry as 鮓.

items.name: Rockfish, sculpin, or goby
items.meaning: A fish kanji with Japanese readings for rockfish, sculpin or bullhead, freshwater goby, and a rare shellfish sense.
items.note: The gori reading is listed as probably the most common, while kochi and mate are rare uses.

items.name: Gun or giant fish
items.meaning: Gun, a figure in Chinese mythology; historical dictionaries also gloss the character as a giant fish.
items.note: The Japanese entry lists only the on reading, so the public meaning follows the sourced Chinese definitions.

items.name: Goby or shark
items.meaning: Goby in Japanese usage, with same also listed as a shark reading and shark as the Chinese gloss.
items.note: The Japanese noun section documents haze as goby, while Chinese usage documents shark.

items.name: Octopus
items.meaning: Octopus; an alternate fish-radical spelling read tako in Japanese.
items.note: Although written with the fish radical, octopus is a mollusk; compare the reviewed 鱆 entry.

items.name: Ray or dolphin
items.meaning: Ray or skate in Chinese; a rare Japanese spelling for dolphin or porpoise read iruka.
items.note: The Japanese iruka use is rare, and dolphins and porpoises are mammals despite the fish radical.

items.name: Japanese littleneck clam
items.meaning: Japanese littleneck clam or Manila clam; a shellfish read asari in Japanese.
items.note: Although written with the fish radical, the main Japanese gloss is a clam.

items.name: Herring roe
items.meaning: Herring roe; fish eggs known as kazunoko in Japanese.

items.name: Flathead
items.meaning: Flathead; a fish known as kochi in Japanese.
items.note: 鮴 is listed as an alternative spelling for this kochi sense.

items.name: Dried squid
items.meaning: Dried squid or cuttlefish; a seafood foodstuff known as surume in Japanese.
items.note: The dried squid sense is now most common; older usage can also refer to Japanese flying squid.

items.name: Salamander
items.meaning: Salamander; also a kanji sense for female whale and small fish.
items.note: Although written with the fish radical, the main Japanese noun sense is a salamander.

items.name: Mythical giant fish
items.meaning: A giant fish from Chinese mythology, known in Japanese with the on reading kon.
items.note: The Japanese noun entry describes a northern-sea giant fish from Chinese mythology.

items.name: Flathead mullet
items.meaning: Flathead mullet; a coastal fish known as bora in Japanese.
items.note: The fish has size- and region-dependent names: todo for large adults, ina for juveniles, and nayoshi as an archaic name.

items.name: Herring
items.meaning: Herring; also a kanji sense for roe or fish eggs.
items.note: The Japanese entry lists 鰊 as a synonym for the herring sense and 鮞 for the roe sense.

items.name: Horse mackerel
items.meaning: Horse mackerel; another kanji form used for aji.

items.name: Loach
items.meaning: Loach; a freshwater fish read dojo in Japanese.
items.note: The character is kept separate from the reviewed 鰌 entry even though both are read dojo.

items.name: Orca or shachihoko
items.meaning: Orca or killer whale; also shachihoko, a tiger-headed carp-like roof ornament in Japanese mythology.
items.note: A kokuji; the shachi sense is an orca, while shachihoko is a mythical roof ornament.

items.name: Catfish
items.meaning: Catfish, especially Amur catfish; also an idiomatic earthquake sense in Japanese.
items.note: A kokuji; the biological term is often written in katakana as ナマズ.

items.name: Shrimp or prawn
items.meaning: Shrimp, prawn, lobster, crayfish, or another long-tailed decapod crustacean read ebi in Japanese.
items.note: An alternative spelling of 海老; although written with the fish radical, the main Japanese sense is a crustacean.

items.name: Codfish
items.meaning: Codfish; a rare alternative spelling of 鱈 read tara.
items.note: The source lists 鰔 as a rare alternative spelling for the tara or codfish entry; karei is also listed as a kun reading.

items.name: Higai fish
items.meaning: Higai; a Japanese fish name identified with Sarcocheilichthys variegatus.
items.note: The kanji entry also lists sturgeon and carp senses, while the Japanese noun entry gives higai.

items.name: Gill
items.meaning: Gill; the breathing organ of fish and other aquatic animals.
items.note: Alternative spellings listed in the source include 腮 and 顋.

items.name: Pond loach
items.meaning: Pond loach; an alternative spelling of 泥鰌 read dojo.
items.note: The source treats 鰌 as a variant of 鰍 in Chinese and as an alternative spelling of 泥鰌 in Japanese.

items.name: Japanese Spanish mackerel
items.meaning: A spring-associated fish commonly eaten in Japan.
items.note: Often written in katakana as サワラ in biological contexts.

items.name: Righteye flounder
items.meaning: Righteye flounder; a flatfish known as karei in Japanese.
items.note: The Japanese entry also lists a rare obsolete sole sense through 舌鮃.

items.name: Abalone or blowfish
items.meaning: Abalone in dated usage, or blowfish; read awabi or fugu in Japanese.
items.note: The abalone sense is dated, and abalone is a mollusk despite the fish radical.

items.name: Pacific herring
items.meaning: Pacific herring; a fish read nishin or regionally kado in Japanese.
items.note: The nishin entry lists 鯡 as an alternative spelling; kado is chiefly Tohoku usage.

items.name: Kairagi sharkskin
items.meaning: Kairagi; rough shark or ray skin used as a material, and by extension a sharkskin-like ceramic glaze.
items.note: Kairagi can also be written 梅花皮.

items.name: Sardine
items.meaning: Sardine or Japanese pilchard; a kyujitai form read iwashi in Japanese.
items.note: Kyujitai form of 鰛 and an alternative spelling of 鰯.

items.name: Sardine
items.meaning: Sardine or Japanese pilchard; a shinjitai form read iwashi in Japanese.
items.note: Shinjitai form of 鰮 and an alternative spelling of 鰯.

items.name: Widower
items.meaning: Widower; a non-fish social term written with the fish radical.
items.note: Chinese definitions also include an obsolete yellowcheek fish sense, but the Japanese entry centers on widower.

items.name: Japanese amberjack
items.meaning: Japanese amberjack or yellowtail; the adult fish known as buri in Japanese.
items.note: The source notes size- and region-dependent names for this fish; kamasu is listed as a separate barracuda spelling sense.

items.name: Striped mullet
items.meaning: Striped or flathead grey mullet; a fish read bora in Japanese.
items.note: Often written 鯔; 鰡 also appears in 鰡子, karasumi or dried mullet roe.

items.name: Sailfin sandfish
items.meaning: Sailfin sandfish; a fish known as hatahata in Japanese.
items.note: 鱩 is listed as an alternative spelling; ina is listed as a nanori reading.

items.name: Anglerfish
items.meaning: Anglerfish; the ko component of anko in 鮟鱇.
items.note: Often encountered as the second character of 鮟鱇, read anko.

items.name: Mythological sea turtle
items.meaning: Huge mythological sea turtle; read oogame or go in Japanese.
items.note: Although written with the fish radical, the sourced meaning is a mythological turtle, not a fish.

items.name: Octopus
items.meaning: Octopus; a seafood mollusk read tako in Japanese.
items.note: Although written with the fish radical, octopus is a mollusk.

items.name: Swim bladder
items.meaning: Swim bladder; a buoyancy organ in fish read ukibukuro in Japanese.
items.note: Alternative spellings listed in the source include 浮き袋, 浮袋, and 浮き囊.

items.name: Sillaginid or whiting
items.meaning: Sillaginid or whiting; a fish known as kisu in Japanese.

items.name: Namasu
items.meaning: Namasu; a dish of raw fish or vegetables dressed with vinegar.
items.note: Kanjipedia also lists 膾 as an alternative spelling.

items.name: Daggertooth pike conger
items.meaning: Daggertooth pike conger; an eel-like fish known as hamo in Japanese.
items.note: The older hamu reading is listed as archaic or possibly obsolete.

items.name: Large shark
items.meaning: Large shark; a general shark term read fuka in Japanese.
items.note: The source also lists a Chinese variant relationship to 鯗, meaning dried or cured fish.

items.name: Japanese sea bass
items.meaning: Japanese sea bass; a fish known as suzuki in Japanese.
items.note: The source notes that organism names like this are often written in katakana in biological contexts.
